workItem.title: Bound durable audit volume and recover legacy outboxes

## Context

Symphony persisted high-frequency Codex App Server deltas as durable audit events, producing thousands of outbox files, GitHub rate-limit pressure, and excessive memory use. Existing legacy outboxes must be recovered without deleting durable lifecycle, command, or tool evidence.

## Expected outcome

Durable audit captures lifecycle, command, and tool evidence at bounded volume; legacy telemetry is quarantined recoverably; pending durable events rebase idempotently and the X1 orchestrator remains healthy.

## Scope

- Restrict durable App Server audit events to turn lifecycle plus command and tool boundaries
- Redact command and environment details according to audit policy
- Quarantine non-durable legacy telemetry without deleting recoverable files
- Normalize legacy turn-start events and preserve hash-chain rebasing
- Validate recovery, idempotency, memory behavior, and full Elixir quality gates
- Deploy the verified runner binary and observe production recovery

## Out of scope

- Persisting token deltas or streamed message fragments as permanent audit events
- Deleting historical outbox data

## Acceptance criteria

- Full Symphony quality gate passes with 100 percent test coverage and zero Dialyzer errors
- Legacy telemetry is moved to a recoverable quarantine and durable command, tool, and turn events remain pending
- Restarting the X1 service does not recreate an unbounded outbox or memory spike
- Pending durable events flush idempotently through game-api and GitHub
- No secrets, environment values, or unredacted command payloads are persisted
